blocks.summary: Explored vectorized execution in JS. Found: (1) virtual dispatch is main bottleneck, not cache effects; (2) 5.8x speedup for deep 5-operator pipelines; (3) no benefit for GROUP BY; (4) requires columnar format — row objects kill benefit; (5) hybrid Volcano-Vectorized design is best approach (drop-in Iterator replacement that batches internally). Wrote scratch note.

blocks.task: Explore full-stack MicroGPT training: backprop through transformer blocks, not just output proj
blocks.summary: Explored full-stack MicroGPT training. Full backprop works (loss 4.93→4.30 in 10 epochs) but converges slower than output-proj-only (4.40→2.04 in 50 epochs). Key insight: output-proj-only training is a linear probe on frozen features — fast convergence, good for demos. Full backprop needs AdamW + warmup + many epochs. This matches known ML behavior.

blocks.task: Profile Sudoku solving bottleneck - 8.4s seems slow for CDCL, find optimization opportunities
blocks.summary: Profiled Sudoku bottleneck: 3.17M propagations, 70K conflicts in 4.8s = 666K prop/sec. Bottleneck is watched-literal scanning in JS vs C (MiniSat does ~10M prop/sec). Implementation is correct MiniSat-style 2WL. 50x gap is expected JS-vs-C overhead. Micro-optimizations (TypedArrays, less allocation) could help but wont close the gap.

blocks.task: End-to-end: train MoE to learn multi-modal function with AdamW, verify expert specialization
blocks.summary: End-to-end MoE + AdamW training: piecewise function f(x)=x² if x>0, -x if x≤0. 4 experts topK=1: Expert 0 specializes on negative inputs, Expert 3 on positive. Perfect routing separation. Loss 2.3→0.008. Predictions within 0.05 of ground truth. This demonstrates the full MoE→AdamW pipeline works with real expert specialization.
